content:
On Sunday morning, several low-flying, slow-moving small objects were detected in Finnish airspace over sea areas and in Southeast Finland. The Air Force responded immediately and sent an F/A-18 Hornet fighter to identify the objects. According to current information, one drone crashed north of Kouvola and another east of Kouvola. Police have secured both areas for further investigation.

Defense Minister Antti Häkkänen said that drones had entered Finnish territory and that the matter is being taken very seriously, adding that security authorities responded immediately. The Defence Forces are investigating the incident together with other authorities.
